name: Frazer Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering checking, savings, CDs, and loans with modern digital banking and Kasasa rewards accounts across Oklahoma locations.
description_long: Frazer Bank is a community bank with FDIC insurance backing and membership, operating multiple branches across Oklahoma including Edmond, Altus, Frederick, and Hobart. The bank positions itself as combining modern banking conveniences with personalized community bank service, leveraging digital tools while maintaining local presence and customer relationships. The institution offers a comprehensive range of personal and business banking products, from basic checking and savings accounts to certificates of deposit, retirement accounts, and various loan products including mortgages, installment loans, and lines of credit. Frazer Bank distinguishes itself through partnerships like Kasasa rewards checking programs, which offer cash rewards and ATM fee refunds to qualifying customers, positioning it as more than a traditional community bank. The bank provides robust digital banking services including online banking, Zelle money transfer, mobile debit cards, and financial tools like calculators and account switching assistance. Their business services are notably robust, including agricultural lending, construction loans, and cash management services tailored to small business and farm operations. Frazer Bank is best suited for Oklahoma residents seeking community-oriented banking with rewards features and local decision-making, though their geographic footprint appears limited to specific Oklahoma cities. The main limitation is their regional scope—primarily serving Oklahoma—making them inaccessible to customers outside their service areas.
services: Free Kasasa Cash Checking with rewards and ATM fee refunds
Free Kasasa Saver automated savings accounts
Personal and business certificates of deposit (CDs)
Individual retirement accounts (IRAs) and business retirement accounts
Mortgage loans including home purchase and refinance
Personal installment loans and Redi-Credit lines of credit
Business loans, agricultural loans, and construction/interim financing
Business checking accounts with interest-bearing options
Online banking and business online banking platforms
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full faith and credit of U.S. Government backing
Free Kasasa Cash checking with monthly rewards and nationwide ATM fee refunds when qualifying
Multiple account options across personal (4+ checking types) and business banking (3+ checking types)
Comprehensive loan products including agricultural loans and construction/interim financing tailored to rural/business needs
Zelle integration for secure peer-to-peer money transfers
Free financial education resources including online education center, fraud prevention, and financial literacy materials
Business cash management services available for small business operations
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to specific Oklahoma cities (Edmond, Altus, Frederick, Hobart)—not accessible nationally
Website mentions serious security vulnerability in user's browser at time of access, indicating potential site security awareness issues
Limited transparency on specific APR rates, fees, or minimum balance requirements in publicly available website content
No indication of mobile app availability or modern neobank features despite positioning as 'modern bank'
best_for: Oklahoma residents seeking community-focused banking with rewards checking and local branch access
Small business owners and agricultural operations needing specialized lending products and cash management services
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance and community relationship banking over national bank convenience
diagnosis: Frazer Bank is best for Oklahoma residents and small business/agricultural operators seeking community-oriented FDIC-insured banking with rewards checking and specialized lending. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to four Oklahoma cities, making it inaccessible to customers outside these service areas.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
